raw_consumer_statement:
A charge in the amount of {$1600.00} was placed on our card XX/XX/XXXX by Barclays. The company indicated was XXXX XXXX XXXX. The phone number on the XXXX statement was a recording at XXXX telling you to check their site for transaction receipts which I did and of course, there wasn't XXXX because we purchased nothing from this company. 

Initially, the dispute was ignored, and it took several more calls to get Barclays to actually open an investigation. We have opened XXXX disputes since with Barclays. Each one has been denied because they received an invoice from a person - not a company- and I was told because it has my email address on it, they consider it a valid invoice. After the first denial, it has taken them less than an hour to deny the other cases. I can only assume they are not doing anything additional to investigate. 

They finally provided me with a phone which is for someone other than the name on the invoice. We were able to locate a total of XXXX phone numbers for this individual. I have texted and called and received no response back. We have requested a copy of the invoice from Barclays and have yet to receive a copy. I was even told by XXXX representative that because my husband did not answer the phone when they called, they denied the claim and closed it. 

Barclays lost information from first claim filed in XXXXXXXX XXXX  # XXXX XX/XX/XXXX - XXXX XXXX # XXXX XX/XX/XXXX - XXXX XX/XX/XXXX - XXXX sending invoice and reopening case XXXX XXXX The last phone call with them was on XX/XX/XXXX. I was told a new case would be open. Within an hour it was closed. They don't seem to understand, everyone 's information is on the internet. 

We did not make this purchase. It is fraud and Barclays unwillingness to do anything amounts to legalized theft. I have no evidence I did not purchase the supposed services because I did not purchase them, and Barclays keeps telling me I must prove I didn't purchase anything.
